mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that allows users to manage security, compliance, and governance through policy-as-code. It is particularly useful for platform engineering teams looking to enforce policies across their Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with policy definitions and potential performance impacts when enforcing complex policies.

steps.title: Add the Kyverno Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kyverno Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno

```

> This is the official repository for Kyverno.

steps.title: Install Kyverno with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Kyverno in the 'kyverno' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kyverno

helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

```

> Create a namespace for Kyverno.
> Install Kyverno with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify Kyverno installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kyverno pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Ensure all Kyverno pods are in 'Running' state.

steps.title: Create an example policy
steps.description:
This step creates a sample policy to demonstrate Kyverno's functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: 'Label `app` is required.'
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: '?*'
EOF

```

> This policy enforces that all Pods must have an 'app' label.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: 'Label `app` is required.'
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: '?*'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the policy is working
steps.description:
This step checks if the policy is enforced by creating a Pod without the required label.

```bash

kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --restart=Never --namespace default

kubectl get events --sort-by='.metadata.creationTimestamp'

```

> This Pod should be denied due to the missing 'app' label.
> Check the events to see the policy enforcement message.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kyverno from the cluster. Note that any policies created will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno

kubectl delete namespace kyverno

```

> This command uninstalls Kyverno.
> This command deletes the Kyverno namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing policies, upgrading Kyverno, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusterpolicies -o yaml > backup-policies.yaml

helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --version 1.17.1

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Backup existing policies before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all Kyverno pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'validation failed' when creating a resource
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The resource does not comply with the enforced policies.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the resource meets the requirements defined in the policies.

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kyverno pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kyverno' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Policy not being enforced
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy may not be applied correctly or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the policy configuration and ensure it is applied using 'kubectl get clusterpolicies'.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to the Kyverno service
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check network policies and service configurations to ensure proper access.

versionNotes.changes: Improved policy validation performance, added support for new Kubernetes resource types.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kyverno pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n kyverno' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:

  rules:
    - name: check-labels

        pattern:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
